text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. One effective way is to use SMART goals, which are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," you might set "increase website traffic by 20% in three months." Next, break the project into smaller milestones, like completing a design review or finishing the first prototype. This makes progress easier to track. You also need to define specific deliverables, such as reports or software features, so everyone knows what to produce. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with all team members and clients. This way, everyone shares the same vision and expectations, reducing confusion and improving collaboration.
